task_completion.description: Select or navigate to the 'Standard' calendar record
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Standard' calendar is visible and selected in the Calendar window or tree view.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of a new Year record
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new, blank Year record form is opened under the selected Standard calendar.
The text '2030' is entered into the 'Name' input field of the Year record.

task_completion.description: Set the Fiscal Year field to '2030'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text '2030' is entered into the 'Year' or 'Fiscal Year' input field.

task_completion.description: Trigger the process to generate accounting periods
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Create Periods' button is clicked, or a process dialog confirming the generation of periods is visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that 12 standard monthly accounting periods were generated
task_completion.visual_evidence: A list, grid, or tree view showing exactly 12 monthly periods (e.g., Jan-Dec) associated with the newly created 2030 year record.

integrity.description: Agent used the application GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the iDempiere web interface, not terminal commands, API calls, or direct database queries.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with actual form fields
integrity.visual_evidence: Inputs like '2030' are typed into the proper iDempiere application fields, not into a text editor or a mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent actually created the record rather than viewing an existing one
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a progression from an empty form or creation dialog to a saved record with generated periods, proving the agent performed the creation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target year is 2030. According to iDempiere documentation, the 'Create Periods' process automatically generates exactly 12 standard calendar month long periods. Verify that the year 2030 is created and exactly 12 periods are successfully generated.
